reason: Keep native taskbar, tray, persistence, and window lifecycle inside the desktop backend boundary.
Native transition failures must not commit misleading menu or renderer state.
Apply the Rust and cross-layer quality gates to new commands, events, and Windows-only code.
Place the second projection, desktop adapter, hook, and types in existing ownership layers.
Validate desktop preferences and projection events at the Tauri renderer boundary.
Keep taskbar rendering presentational, bounded, accessible, and action-driven.
Follow the established late-listener cleanup and hydration patterns.
Preserve fixed logical dimensions, strict TypeScript, focused tests, and high-DPI checks.
The feature spans native host state, IPC/events, two render projections, and persistence.
Use the independently documented Win32 taskbar mechanism and licensing boundary.
Use the independently documented NSStatusItem behavior and Tauri tray-title adaptation.
